reviews.review_text:
We stayed here recently and overall it was an okay stay, but there are definitely some things that need improving.

The biggest issue was the powered/water sites. It seems like around 5–6 sites are sharing the same power and water access point, and depending on where you’re positioned, some people can’t properly access the power or water they’ve paid for.

Another issue was the grey water drain pipe being blocked. Instead of it being fixed, people were being told to run their grey water to random trees, which unfortunately caused some pretty bad smells around nearby campsites.

We also had an unpleasant interaction with the manager. There seemed to be some miscommunication regarding having two cars on our site, as we had previously been told it would be okay. We completely understand policies need to be followed, but the way it was handled came across quite rude and unprofessional, including threats to remove everyone from the site if another car was seen. A calmer and more understanding approach would have gone a long way.

We understand the cyclone has likely caused disruptions and damage, and we do appreciate that. However, some of these issues seem like things that could be managed or maintained a bit better moving forward. The location itself has potential, but the overall experience could definitely be improved.

Outdated facilities, dangerous and rusty playground and OMG FREEZING POOL!!!
Dusty and dirty and suppose to be able to use some electricity, but put kettle on, no power, microwave on, no power!
Booked 4 nights, stayed 1👎
“Old prison facility” named “ Monopoly”

We had an excellent seven nights stay at Yardie Station. The girls at reception were fantastic especially Lourdes and. Chloe.
They also do Dog Minding, which is fantastic to get into the national park or out on a boat.
Definitely the place to stay rather than in Exmouth.
Thanks heaps, ladies.
Great place to launch to the Ningaloo reef. Good facilities and well run station stay.

Clean facilities. Handy having water & power.

Not on the beach but close to the amazing beaches.

Nice camping set up, perfect if you have a boat, the ramp is about 2km away. Has everything you need except ac. They generate thier own electricity so anything with a heavy draw cannot be used. But this is a minor inconvenience for such a lovely place.  Great for the beaches and also so the Yardie creek  boat tour, highly recommended.
Had the best time

The campsite is in a quiet location with plenty of trees (providing shade) and clean sanitary facilities (although they are a bit dated). We were here six weeks after the cyclone and while the effects weren't noticeable, it was still lovely. The Milky Way is perfectly visible from here.

The only good thing about this place is the location. Please feel free to see the photos attached. We booked a powered site for $60, called and double-checked with staff because we were going to arrive after office hours, everything was OK. When we arrived, upon checking out the campsite, we realised the power was actually 2 sites down, so in order to connect anything we needed a 10-15 meter lead, wich we did not have. We were told by other campers that the reception has leads to lend to campers but as we arrived after office hours and the lady I spoke to on the phone twice didn't tell me, we were left without power, for $60 a night. The "kitchen" and bbq areas are disgusting to say the least. Only 1 working very gross sink, 2 bbqs in horrible condition, no roof, no utensils of any kind. Please see photos. The bathrooms were dirty too and many parts of the camping smelled like sewage. It's insulting to campers that they would charge anything really for the conditions they offer. I highly doubt the owners of this place use bathrooms or a kitchen like the one the offer to their customers. If you go to the Pony Club campsite, you won't be offered a lot, but you'll pay half, which is was more honest than this terrible place. It is known travelers are not welcomed in Exmouth but offering camping conditions like the ones offered by Yardie Homestead is embarrasing to say the least.

An incredible location with incredibly helpful staff!

Boat washing station as you drive into camp, fish cleaning station as well. Just be sure to take your offals with you.

Less than a five minute drive to the boat ramp which makes it the perfect location or base for your fishing adventures!

Its a large and sandy campground with enough space for caravan and vehicle. Very close to the boat ramp for the various tours or fishing.. Bathrooms are pretty dated. Pool closed at 5.30 which was too early on very hot humid days. On the whole, it was a good stay.

Very disappointed with this one... After having to cancel our booking with them due to cyclone Narelle - we had booked for 08/04 , they told us that they will not refund us for all the days as they were reopened on the 9th... All area was damaged and pretty sure campground would have been too... They said they have a 35 day cancellation policy and though we called on the 4th, asked them if they are opened, they said no, suggested to cancel as they still didn't know, they are refusing to refund us the full amount and only refunded 1 day that they were still closed... We seem to be paying $500 for something that we never used and was not our intention to cancel but an unpredictable weather condition that had everyone on their feet and Emergency services were evacuating everyone that was not a resident from all affected areas, sending us 300km off cyclones road. We evacuated from Denham and advised to go inland to Mt magnet or Newman, both around 8-9 hours drive inland... I am going to get advise from Consumers affairs as this was just unacceptable.😡😡😡

Nice but really expensive and a lot of people reverse they poop fluid in to the grey water in the night the management should check .... Room not so big and the electrical connector was not working pool close at 5.15 pm
Sehr freundlicher Empfang bei der Rezeption. Ganzer Platz ist sehr sauber, und bietet große Stellplätze. Guter Ausgangspunkt für die verschiedenen Schnorchelplätze. Würden jederzeit wieder kommen.

Der Campingplatz ist Mitten im Nirgendwo und keine Zivilisation in der Nähe.

Einziger Vorteil: Er ist nah am Nationalpark.

Leider können wir diesen Campingplatz nicht empfehlen. Wir haben uns von der modernen Website und den schönen Bildern auf der Website blenden lassen. Der Campingplatz sieht leider überhaupt nicht mehr so aus. Das Café/ Restaurant am Poolbereich gibt es scheinbar schon seit Jahren nicht mehr, hier sah zumindest nichts danach aus, das hier in letzter Zeit bewirtet wurde oder Live-Musik stattgefunden hat.

WCs und Duschen sind sehr dreckig und uralt. Eine richtige Campkitchen gibt es nicht nur Outdoor BBQ Plätze, zwei Gas-Kochplatten und ein Spülbecken, jedoch ohne Beleuchtung oder Überdachung. Es gibt keinerlei elektronische Geräte (kein Toaster, Ofen, Wasserkocher o.ä.). Diese dürfen auch nicht benutzt werden im Camper. Klimaanlage darf auch nicht genutzt werden, was bei den Temperaturen nachts eine Zumutung ist.

Leider waren die Alternativen nach dem Cyclone überschaubar. Die Campingplätze im Nationalpark waren alle noch geschlossen, ebenso wie der Ningaloo Caravan Park in Exmouth. Ich denke der RAC wäre eine bessere Alternative.

Fazit: Für eine Nacht geht es, aber erwartet nicht so viel. Aber der Preis ist für diese Ausstattung zu hoch (90 Dollar pro Nacht).
Booked 4 months for in advance for 4 nights only to be given the site closest to fish cleaning station, when this was our one request not to be near this. Smelly, noisy and busy. Very disappointed.

This is a last-resort kind of place. It's packed, and the facilities are incredibly rundown. The only way they make money is when the national park campgrounds are closed and they can justify charging $63 a night to pitch your own tent.

For a place to stay out in the middle of nowhere with quick access to Cape Range Park, this place does good. It's pretty rough and you can't connect full time to water or run a/c or microwave (or anything with a heating element), but just manage your expectations considering how far away this location is from civilization, and you'll have a great time. Fishing is good off the beach in the area as well.
Great location excellent facilities 🍾

I had a site booking with this mob. Had to cancel due to sickness. No refund as it was not within the 35 days required. Think that is ruff. You don’t know what’s going to happen. Offered nothing. $230 gone. Very bad. Don’t go there people.
Great place, located well to all spots and good amenities

Wir hatten eine Nacht auf diesem Campingplatz gebucht und konnten dann leider nicht kommen, da wir aufgrund des Zyklons in den Süden fahren mussten und auch später die Straßen gesperrt waren. Nach mehrmaligen kontaktieren für eine Zurückerstattung kamen von den 60$ nur 10$ an, das ist eine Frechheit, denn 50$ wären wohl eine Buchungsgebühr. Alle anderen Campingplätze und Aktivitäten haben uns den vollständigen Betrag erstattet, insbesondere, da es eine Situation ist, auf die man keine Einfluss hat.
Clean facilities, well maintained ground. Only gripe is the fish cleaning station is right next to the tent area. No way to really avoid the fishy smell.

I do not recommend this place. power restrictions i get but you have to switch your inverter on to use microwave,  induction,  coffee machine,   air fryer. When the flies are in your face, you don't want to bbq outside... facilities need attention, i.e., tennis court has no net and surface cracked,   unable to use.. clothesline has one that has a line..  barhrooms dated but clean. location is great but i should have stayed at rac exmouth which has power and water and cheaper rates.
The park is dog friendly but the dog friendly beaches are in town.
.. 7 months travelling and this is the not high on my list.
Nice quite campground

Hi i am 83 years of age and have been to campsite at least 6 times we arrived 27th of dec 25 im afraid the place was dirty and very disappointing the showers where clean but need a good scrub mens toilets had no hot water for 3 days rubbish bins smelly and overful.  NOBODY ON RECEPTION WHEN NEEDED we were asked to be careful with water ok but every shower leaked the grass area that was closed again water was sprinkling for 2 days non stop very disappointing holiday

Wer je eine Immobilie gekauft hat kennt es….
Lage,Lage,Lage…
36€ Mitte November, Pool schließt um 17:30Uhr und die Kirsche auf der Torte…
Bei powered Stellplatz,
„Unser Stromnetz schafft keine Klimaanlage 😂😂😂😂aber wir hätten trotzdem gerne 36€ weil
Lage,Lage,Lage und das stimmt halt!
12Minuten zum Leuchtturm für die Turtels am Abend und 4Minuten zur Rampe von wo aus die Schnorchelboote starten
Nice place, they got everything you need for a peaceful stay in a really cool place.
Perfect location if you want the visit the national park but still need a shower/toilet. Amenities are clean and we enjoyed our stay here for 3 nights.
Dieser Campingplatz hat uns gut gefallen, ruhig gelegen, viele Bäume (Schatten) und saubere Sanitäranlagen (sind zwar etwas in die Jahre gekommen).

My girlfriend and I stated at Yardie Homestead for two night at a powered site at the end of October. We used Yardie home base while we explored Cape Range National Park and the Exmouth area. Staff were very friendly and they had a wide range of amenities. There were also many wild birds around the site, no need for an alarm clock!

Unfriendly reception. Pool out of order. Sink for washing dishes without light.
The crossbar at the end of the clothesline (where the lines are attached) is too low and has no edge protection. I didn't notice this and got a scratch on my head – I'm 1.80 m tall. I've been here twice, the first and last time.

This yardie homestead is totally rubbish, and not keeping their word, i booked for them October 7th to 9th and due of vehicle breakdown issue, we didnt make it on 7th and same day i called Yardie Homestead that we are not able to make it. One of the receptionist said she will help me to reschedule it. And i havento give them a call and after i called them they said  they need a date to rebooked the unused days, told them i have to talk to my wife to confirm what date then the following morning all of the sudden they said sorry they cannot help. Very inconsiderate for what happen even you informed them  earlier.
I will not going to booked anymore in this money hunger site and dont recommend to all.
Beware,!
Great RV stop. Close to Exmouth but also Far enough away. Close to Turtle sanctuary  - a must see
Quite far out, sites had no shelter or shade. Paid 9.50 for 2 scoops of ice-cream and the serving wouldn't even fill up an espresso cup. Facilities were dirty.

Great location, easy access to the national park, beaches and Exmouth township. We stayed in a powered site and while we ran off grid anyway the option was there if needed. Amenities a little run down but again, didn’t bother us as we have our own. Massive big park, kid friendly. Grey water seemed to be blocked on a lot of sites though.

this caravan park is a joke we have young kids and there was 1 nappy that feel down the  side of the bed and was missed, saying we left fish blood in the fridge which we cleaned very well before we left, we left a brand new porta cott as we couldnt fitt it, as if they couldnt store it and maybe charge it out for future booking the lady carried on like a pork chop. we tried to resurect it over the phone we would have been happy to pay for the extra cleaning because it would have been so hard to clean but she hung up on me and wouldnt give me the time of day.

Great location for accessing the coast. We stayed for a few nights- paid for a powered site, which was a waste of money since we were only allowed to power our lights in our van…. No use of electrical items which made a powered site so pointless. (Could not use your own kettle or any electrical appliances so should have just stayed on an unpowered beach camping site)
Toilets were a little outdated and not so clean.
Wifi only worked in one area near reception.
Cool outdoor picnic table area!
It's like an oasis in the desert. Well located, close to the most amazing beaches! And half way to National Park. Nice and helpful staff. Kitchen is quite basic, but toilets are good!
Yardie Homestead is a great place to base yourself while you explore Cape Range National Park (Ningaloo). The park is well maintained.
I bagni sono un po vecchi ma puliti

Disappointing. Upsold us to an expensive powered site without our knowledge and then wouldn’t refund the difference.
Old lady manager was very rude to us- didn’t let us fill our 5L water container because it was after 10am on departure day, and said “it’s people like you that ruin things”. Was absolutely Disgusting. I feel sorry for the staff and travellers who don’t have an option but to interact with that old troll.
Interesting place to stay. Loved the unique toilets and showers. There was live music on the night we were there and that was a bonus.
If you don't have Telstra get wifi. There is no excuse for not having an internet connection!

Great camp, staff were fantastic
Some of the amenities are getting a bit tired but were kept spotless
We stayed a week but could have easily doubled that.
We’ll be back for sure

Good for one night, but the common areas are very run-down. Also, there are no electrical outlets anywhere in the campground unless you pay for a powered site. Very disappointing not to be able to charge my phone for $50 a night.
Rustic. Closer to the reef and walks. Limited power can’t use aircon, microwave and electric hot water. Have to use your own inverter or gas. Can fill water tanks from site taps.

Big, busy and noisy.  When they say powered site it's not totally correct. You can't use electric kettles, toasters, air fryers, hair driers or even air conditioners. What's the use of that in hot weather.  The ablutions block near the office is borderline acceptable, but the old, really old transportable one on the mound is disgraceful.  There has been no money spent in recent years but they reap in the profits during peak seasons.  Stay in Exmouth at one of the better parks, only a few dollars more per day but would be a far better experience.

A great base to explore Cape Range National Park

Yardie Homestead was our eighth stop on a campervan journey from Broome to Perth in July/August 2025. We originally planned to stay longer in Exmouth but managed to get an overflow site at Yardie by calling reception shortly after 9am — and we’re so glad we did!

We had tried to book one of the national park campgrounds when the booking window opened six months in advance, but had no luck. Yardie Homestead turned out to be a fantastic alternative. It’s the only fully equipped campground within Cape Range National Park, offering clean and functional facilities, a small pool, a playground for the kids, and even its own fuel station with reasonable prices.

The atmosphere is much more nature-oriented than in Exmouth, and the location is perfect for visiting the pristine beaches of the national park. The Tantabiddi Boat Ramp — where most boat tours depart during this season — is just a 5-minute drive away.

Highly recommended if you're looking for a comfortable yet close-to-nature base to explore the area!

Fantastic location right near the national park and Exmouth proper.
We stayed for 6 nights for only this reason, but found $50 for an unpowered shared patch of grass was a bit steep. Especially when you aren't guaranteed to be able to park beside your tent setup.
The kitchen was quite disgusting on a daily basis - flies, garbage, food, dirty tables, dirty sinks, etc.
There were two different toilet blocks we used - one was pretty bad and the other was fine.
It was busy when we had arrived and the whole campsite felt very crammed in with little to no privacy.
Cafe is convient but pricey. With no competition in the area, it makes sense they have so much business.

We really enjoyed our 2 weeks at Yardie Homestead on a powered site. The sites are spacious with concrete slab and shared water tap. The showers were hot although the facilities a little tired and could do with an upgrade.
Yardie’s location is great with easy access to world class snorkelling at Oyster Stacks, TurquoiseBay and Lakeside which are all worth going to. It was also very close to great surf beaches and only 28 mins to Exmouth.
One woman on reception and the phone was quite abrupt which is not pleasant, but we wouldn’t let that spoil our stay.
It’s been mentioned on here several times so it’s probably time management gave her some customer service training as it sours it for some people. All of the other women in the office were lovely and very helpful, as were the grounds staff. Bins need emptying more often as there are a lot of blow flies.

Very good service from Claire. All the caravan parks and overflows were booked out, we were going to have to skip Ex-mouth. She managed to get us sorted and we we able to see Whale sharks, Humpbacks, Turtles, Wallabies, Lizards, Many Fish+Birds, Stingrays, Reef Sharks, Fruit Bats, etc. during our stay that we otherwise would have missed.
Whoever gives this Caravan more than 1 star is obviously getting paid or something is not right. Terrible rude staff. Shocking ablutions. I do not recommend.

This is in our opinion a perfect young family camping location which gives you all the basic ammenities. You only have a short drive to do many snorkeling spots and easy drive into Exmouth for any supplies.
Will definitely stay again one day.
Facilities need to be knocked down and remodelled, tired but hot water at least, power charged the caravan battery up at least which was good. Wouldn't go back I don't think

AVOID THIS PLACE - IF YOU HAVE AN ALTERNATIVE

We did not feel welcome here at all. The behavior and rudeness from the staff tainted our entire stay in Exmouth. Unfortunately, the staff were rude or unhelpful on multiple occasions over our stay.

Let me share one example:
After our first night on the site, we went to reception and politely asked if there was a place anywhere on the grounds where we could plug in our phone to charge. The lady at reception was almost delighted to answer in a rude tone that “you booked an unpowered site, and that means UNPOWERED.” It felt as if she heard this question often.

We have traveled through Australia for months, always booking unpowered sites because we camp in a tent and do not have lights or kitchen appliances to power. We have always had access to an outlet in a common area for charging small devices (phone, toothbrush, laptop). When we mentioned this, she admitted that other campgrounds allow it, but insisted that here, they don’t. Her justification: they run their own power system, and electricity is expensive. So we offered to pay separately just to charge a phone, but she shot us down again with a “NO. You could have spent just $10 more for a powered site”. For context, powered sites were not available at the time of booking...

I can absolutely understand if a place has limited power supply and carefully rationed for fridges, heaters, or other large appliances that consume significant electricity. But refusing to let guests charge a single phone? That’s not about electricity. Fully charging an iPhone requires around 0.015 kWh, probably a cent’s worth of power. Instead they have covered up every single outlet in the campground, even disabling the ones in the bathrooms, and are being rude to customers when they ask kindly. Meanwhile, the communal lights (such as the kitchen) stay on all day and all night.

Well, you may have saved $0.05 worth of electricity during our stay, but in return you’ve earned another one-star review; one that could have been so cheaply avoided.

I never have experienced a more unprofessional, unhelpful staff member than Clare. Obviously Mr Forrest has no idea who he employs.|Travel down the road to Bullara Station for a lesson in hospitality, service,  positive experiences.|Good luck with recruiting a replacement for Clare

From the moment we arrived, our experience at this campsite was underwhelming. The two times we approached the staff with simple questions, their responses were unexpectedly rude. Even if everything had been fine otherwise, their attitude alone left a bad impression.

One of the major issues we encountered was with the watering system. Early in the day, I noticed that the grass around our tent was being watered. I immediately spoke to a staff member, who apologized and assured me that the water to our spot would be turned off. I even asked whether I should move the tent, but was told it wasn't necessary.
Later on, we discovered that the sprinklers had been left on, and everything inside our tent, including sleeping bags and other gear, was wet. Nothing dried properly, making for a very uncomfortable night.
On our departure day, we hung up the wet items to dry in the sun. But when we came back to collect them, we were not even allowed to drive into the campsite to pick up our things. Again, the staff showed no flexibility or understanding.

It’s clear that this campsite benefits from having no competition in the area and unfortunately, it shows. If there were other options nearby, we would not return here, and I doubt many others would either.

Thought the park itself and location was amazing. All staff encountered in park grounds were awesome and girls at reception too. There was just one lady in reception was pretty rude to everyone in our group of 6 individually at one point or another over our 5 night stay. Might be time for her to consider getting out of the tourism industry and doing something else if she can’t deal with people properly. Apart from that aspect which really didn’t affect our stay it was awesome.
Yardie basically has it all, section for the kiddies, live music, restaurant/cafe, great facilities and very boat friendly! We will definitely be returning! Thanks guys!
10 dollars for large Coffee....
A great campground situated right near Ningaloo and Cape Range NP.

Fantastic stay at Yardie Homestead.  Sharon and the team were so helpful during our stay.  We had a great site for our large caravan.  Great amenities, cafe meals and entertainment.  Really appreciate such great facilities in such a remote area.  We will be back!|Anni and Gaz

Friendly staff, large raked clean caravan sites with concrete pad,  clean amenities, live music most nights, good food at Cafe/restaurant, close to popular day reef sites for swimming and snorkelling,
Lots of kids. Lots of dust. Surprisingly though very good coffee from the young lady that is doing the cafe this season.

Very rude person in the office. I was asking if they had a spot for me, regarding their special offer 'book 6, stay 7' and she told me she had only two nights available. To my question, why they have that special offer online, if they are fully booked, she answered with a smug smile: "This offer is for people who plan in advance" I've answered that some people don't like to book in advance, and she put her hands up, saying she had only two nights available... They might be friendly to those who book in advance, but not to me.

Staff were very rude on arrival and when we had queries through out our stay. Very unhelpful and unkind.
The lady with the red glasses is the only exception, she was lovely and extremely helpful. Makes all the difference, thank you.

Not accomodating at all. The lady at the front reception is rude and doesn’t want conversation at all. The staff try and get money out you for everything, the facilities need updating and the campground is dirt with a bit of concrete. Would not recommend to other travelers.

Schlechtester Campingplatz!!! Unfreundliches Personal, Toiletten und Duschen alt und renovierungsbedürftig, sehr teuer für unpowered campsite. Da kann man sich einfach nur in die Wildnis stellen und hat nettere Nachbarn und eine schönere Erfahrung. Nicht zu empfehlen!!!! Haben uns als Gruppe junger Mädchen einfach in die Wildnis Australiens ausgesetzt.
Good place to stay.

My review is solely based on how I was treated on my 15 night stay at Yardie Homestead. I always request particular sites to suit my campertrailer which I didn't get. Ended uo on a site where my rig and car only took up half the space. A real waste as a bigger rig could of used it. Even if you book 12 months in advance you are told it can't be guaranteed. $60 a night for a limited powered sites not the best to offer customers either. If you cancel like I was intending to do due to my car broken down and due to it being a major problem there will be no refund I asked if the remainder of my booking over $600 be transferred to next year's booking answer no can't be done. There was no empathy with staffer no  sorry to hear I am having car trouble. I am only going to now do maximum 7 nights as to do more and have something happen it's to much money to loose. I found the staff mmember i spoke to abrupt shooting back answers to my questions to move me on. This information is just to help other campers know what to expect. On a plus side the Homestead is available most of the time has a fuel station and shop. Close to National Park

Cheers Errol

Date: 12/07/2026

Received a phone call by the Manager of Yardie Homestead asking for my credit card details to refund me the deposit from next years booking I made 7 nights down from 14 nights. Manager explained I obviously don’t like staying at the Homestead due to the scaving review I placed on google regarding my experience at the Homestead. I commented back I don’t like it there because of how I was treated. I have been a traveller all my life retired and live in my camper trailer full time wherever I end up. I was then asked by the female Manager not to come back which I am happy to accommodate. Very bad customer service not professional at all. I am glad if anyone has had a good experience happy for any of fellow travellers it wasn’t my experience.

Cheers Errol

Lady at the reception clearly doesn’t want to do her job anymore she was a little rude and not accommodating at all . Facilities where ok but nothing great . But the rest of the staff where really nice

We had a great stay at the Yardie Homestead (specifically the powered campground). I was impressed with how well appointed it is with lots of facilities (coffee shop, pool, volleyball courts etc). I was also pleasantly surprised with the toilet / shower situation - they were private and pretty clean every time I used them. We were fortunate enough to get a spot right next to the power - I'm not sure how it would work if you got a spot on the opposite side of the camp ground though. Amazing location too. Would stay here again if camping in Exmouth.

Awesome friendly staff. Great location.
Loved how sites were cleaned up before each arrival

Great location for access to Cape Range. Super busy park with stressed out front desk staff, made us feel less than welcome at every interaction, soured the experience for us. Beggars can’t be choosers in peak season I guess.

I am not one to ever leave a bad review, but this situation is very called for…

We broke down in Broome (and have been stranded for just over a week with no good news in site…). We decided to cancel our upcoming legs of the trip to give our future caravan sites as much notice as possible in this unforeseen situation.

Upon contacting Yardie to cancel our week long stay and explained our unfortunate situation, we were told we had to give 35 days notice and that refunds aren’t possible for vechile failure (if only I had a crystal ball and could predict that we were going to breakdown).

So, we made our best efforts to find a replacement for our booking, we messaged as soon as we had found a replacement (a few hours before check in for our supposed booking), only to be told we couldn’t change the name as we didn’t reply within 24 hours.

It seems as though they’ve taken it upon themselves to re-book the site, accept another payment from another group and keep our money too… as they knew they would be able to do during peak season.

If you plan on breaking down (who does) make sure you give them notice, or they’ll take your money and double it.

Every other park has been accomodating and understanding of our unforeseeable situation, very disappointing and we will be making sure we spread the word that Yardie isn’t the site for them.

We stayed here on our trip through the west coast as unfortunately we could not find any spots at the National Parks. Extremely overpriced for what is it ($60/night).
BBQ areas are super dirty and old, toilets are okay.
There was one particular lady on the front desk that clearly doesn't like her job as she was very rude to everyone. She made us feel very uncomfortable because answering our questions seemed like a big effort for her. After reading other reviews we were not the first ones feeling this way.
They clearly take advance of the lack of accommodation in the area. Not nice

Lots of comments online about the water, power and prices. Yes it's not cheap but it is actually ok as long as you don't need air conditioning or your microwave. And you have some water storage on board that you can top up. It's a good location and it's well maintained. Sites are a good size and easily accessed. To be fair one men's toilet block is very old and not good......but there are alternatives that are fine. We would definitely go back.

Zero stars based on the first 2-minute interaction at reception. Our vehicle rego and gate access code didn't work on arrival and I was berated by reception for holding up a line of traffic, which I could do nothing about - a failing on their system, yet I was somehow to blame.

Other than that... the site is just about OK, but not friendly. The list of conditions on the back of the site map is very unwelcoming with most items warning that "penalties apply" for breaching these rules. For example, "absolute silence after 10:00pm"

They use a shared water system so you can't remain hooked up.

It's also low power delivery so you can't run anything more than a few milliamps.

No campfires at any time of the year.

There's also CCTV to ensure you don't break any rules.

Needless to say, we will never return and have already started telling our friends & family about this site.

Had a great stay. Such a great location - super close to the snorkelling beaches and warf. Friendly management with lovely community area and relaxing live music for a great vibe. Found the pricing reasonable for the location. Highly recommend this camp ground.
Good location, close to beaches & National Park.

Used to stay there every year until corona came and everyone else went there !!..even cleaned the loos... They do a wonderful job there and fantastic location lots of respect for James for making the place work for people!....great chap🥰
P.s  glad there's a phone tower near there now to check the weather etc there never was back in the day..😄..
Location: 5/5

We had a powered site here and loved it. Not packed in like sardines and the park itself was very well maintained. Close to Turquoise Bay Oyster Stacks Osprey Bay...all great places to go snorkelling.
Camp-site was nice but at the moment very sand flies and mosquito's need lots of repellent and antihistamines for the bites and bite cream.

We are staying at Yardie Homestead in a caravan.  After setting up we encountered a power supply problem. It transpired that there was an issue with the 12V system on the van. Staff from the site, Damien and Clare were extremely helpful. In his own time. Damien and Clare came to assist isolating the problem. They refused a 6 pack, insisting that it was all part of the service. Outstanding!

spent two weeks in a self contained cabin. Basic clean accommodation, cool with warm shower after day in park. Perfect base for access to cape range and Exmouth for supplies.
The cafe has delicious meals if too tired to cook,
Wonderful stay hope to be back
We really appreciate the communication with last minute organization, thanks guys!  Looking forward to our stay

Highly recommend Yardi Homestead campsite . We stayed there for 7 nights in a RV . Showers , toilets & laundry were super clean with a rustic retro vibe . Lovely restaurant on site serving wholesome meals at a reasonable price  in a very relaxed beachside atmosphere .

Bien pour une nuit
Personnel agréable et professionnel.
Sanitaire très bien et plutôt propre, douche chaude 👍🏻
Espaces communs pas très propre et équipement dur à utiliser.
Pas de prise électrique à disposition dans le camping, il faut prendre un power site.
Durant notre road trip nous faisons principalement des free camp excepté quand nous avons besoin de faire une machine ou de charger nos appareils nécessitant une prise secteur (pc, appareil photo..) que nous ne pouvons pas charger dans notre voiture.
Dommage pour nous il pleuvait et pas d'endroit abrité pour le linge et aucune prise électrique à disposition...
un peu déçu de pas pouvoir charger mes appareils pour 50$ la nuit....
Great location and good facilities, but very poor service at the reception.
Good place to stop
Very enjoyable stay. Relaxed place with all amenities and plenty of space.

Great location to set up your tent or park your caravan. A short drive to the beautiful Turquoise Bay to do the snorkel drift. |Yardie Homestead has live entertainment during the week on certain nights. Fuel is available on site. |Power usage is restricted to low draw appliances and will trip out if you exceed the low power draw limits. |The staff are friendly and the grounds are kept clean and tidy. This was our second stay, we will be back again 😊

Besser man kocht nicht in der Campküche.Das möchte man wirklich nicht- sehr unschön....Der Pool is erfrischend und das Cafe/Restaurant ist wirklich nett und die Leute freundlich.
Lage is gut ---!!!!!!Fliegennetze mitnehmen!!!! Soviel hatten wir no nirgends.Bis auf die grausame Campküche, war es auch ganz  sauber-bissl teuer für das, aber das is wohl die Lage.

Always a lovely stay, super clean and very friendly staff helpful and want the best experience for their guests. We had a problem with the fridge and within a few hours they had gone into town and replaced it with a new one! Sad to leave!!!!

We stayed here 6 years ago and nothing has been spent on the place since then Out dated over priced no hot water in camp kitchen. Staff don’t seem happy Do not recommend better along the beach.  No wonder it was empty

We stayed in a cabin. Absolutely loved it. Had everything we needed. Beds were very comfy. Everything was very clean despite the dusty surroundings. I can’t fault a thing, but then we are realistic regards rural accommodation. I suspect those who have not been so accepting should maybe have stayed at a resort in Exmouth. We used the laundry and camp toilets as well, which were also clean. |Food was great at the cafe - we went burger night and the homemade burgers were lovely. My friend and husband really enjoyed the veggie option.|Sharon was very friendly and helpful from when I phoned to book, to check in, to cooking/serving in the cafe, to check out.|Thank you! We will be back.|Donna and Al.

We had booked in for 3 nights in May.We liked it so much we extended our stay to 5 nights.Why.? Staff were extremely helpful.
Environment was very peaceful with very generous sites. Ready access to drinking water. Swimming pool , cafe and tennis court were available.Water in showers was desalinated. Ready access to Cape Range National park and numerous nearby beaches with excellent snorkling were additional attractions. It was definitely one of the best homesteads we experienced on our trip.
We definitely recommend it.

This is a great spot if you want to be close to the National Park—the location really can’t be beat. We camped here for six nights, but since the campground was full, we ended up at the very end of the site. That area only had one large kitchen, with no fridges and no electricity, which was a bit inconvenient.

Unfortunately, the toilets and showers near our area were never cleaned during our entire stay. It seemed like the facilities closer to the main kitchen were better maintained—they had fridges. Despite the difference in quality, we all paid the same price, which felt a bit unfair.

The office is only open from 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM, and since it’s the only place where you can buy fuel, those hours are quite limiting. It meant we had to leave the beach early just to make it back in time.

Another downside: the rubbish bins weren’t emptied for nearly four days, which became quite unpleasant in the heat. Overall, the location is excellent, but some basic improvements would really enhance the experience.

Wir haben 60 Dollar bezahlt für ne Power Side. Dazu muss man wissen das es nur solar Strom ist und man nicht die Klimaanlage benutzen darf. Toiletten und Duschen sind uralt und echt runtergekommen. Personal wirkt sehr lustlos und haben keinerlei Ortskenntnisse. Der Pool ist super winzig. Das einzige warum der Standort 3 Sterne bekommen hat ist das man es von hier nicht weit zu den einfach absolut wunderschönen Schnorchelstellen hat.

Location is ok, but that’s it. Everything else is horrific. They charge prices they think they can get away with, and if they keep it up surely they won’t last.

Run by a team with poor service, minimal maintenance ability and who only seek to part you from your $$$.

Glued the power points in the shower block so you can’t use! Wow!

I’ve camped in many many places and this is with out doubt the worst place I have been.

Given the location this could be one on the BEST places, but sadly it’s the opposite.

Had car issues all packed up less than hour late to leave our site even after I'd called to let them no as we had head into town to borrow a car to tow out van.  They had clearly mentioned no one was booked on that site - still charged us $15 late check out and we were less than hour over check out time.

Pool was merky. One lady as we checked in on reception was nice then management awful.

Will be letting all our our friends travelling around know  to avoid this place. After all dramas we had the had absolutely no empathy and were greedy charging $15 for patch dirt.

We have stayed in million caravam parks and never experienced such awful management!
Lovely camping ground. A little bit of shade on most sites. The cafe was great. You can’t use your air conditioner as power is limited there. Pool was refreshing and looked clean.

We waren hier gedurende de schoolvakantie. De NP waren allemaal volgeboekt, maar dit was een prima alternatief. |Grote camping met veel verschillende soorten plekken. |We konden hier ook opgehaald worden om een whaleshark tour te doen. |Camping was lang niet vol toen wij er waren. Sanitair toen schoon, weet niet hoe het is als alles vol staat.|Er is een restaurant, en een (heel klein) zwembad. |Leuke kleine "papegaaien" op de camping

big spacious campingground, about 25minutes drive from exmouth, 10 - 40 minutes from cape range (depending on where you wanna go:). it‘s remote and beautiful with the red ground and green trees. facilities are quite run down, but usable and were mostly clean👍🏼

Had paid for a 4 day stay but due to weather further south were unable to make booking due road closures - Contacted and explained and requested credit but advised it was not their problem - Did not expect a refund but thought credit or part credit might be possible
Was fine for our needs. Just have a small camper so was put in one of the short sites. No probs. Power, as others have said, is not for running aircon. Clean toilets and showers. Nice quiet location.

I’ve stayed across a variety of unpowered and powered sites at Yardie - the team at reception are all too happy to help, the park is quiet and away from the busy caravan parks. Close to the snorkel sites & not too far from town.

It's a beautiful space to connect with the environment. You must bring your own food; the place has a fridge and different spaces depending on your campsite.  Kids and pets are allowed. There are shared bathrooms and a shared kitchen. There is also a pool.

The owner was extremely rude, and we didn’t feel welcomed at all. Upon arrival, we were met with a continuous explanation of strict rules, which made us uncomfortable. On the day of checkout, they shut off the water early in the morning for major renovations without any prior notice.||Additionally, the air conditioning was inadequate for the very hot temperatures. It was small, outdated, and not available in every room, leaving one room completely without air conditioning. We had specifically asked about air conditioning before booking, but they failed to mention this issue.||The supplies provided were basic and old, further adding to the disappointment. Overall, it was not an enjoyable experience.

The owner was extremely rude, and we didn’t feel welcomed at all. Upon arrival, we were met with a continuous explanation of strict rules, which made us uncomfortable. On the day of checkout, they shut off the water early in the morning for major renovations without any prior notice.

Additionally, the air conditioning was inadequate for the very hot temperatures. It was small, outdated, and not available in every room, leaving one room completely without air conditioning. We had specifically asked about air conditioning before booking, but they failed to mention this issue.

The supplies provided were basic and old, further adding to the disappointment. Overall, it was not an enjoyable experience.

No good!  After you have paid ,but want to change the date. Even in low season. That should not be an issue!

You absolutely have to be flexible with people after they have paid.  Straight NO is like , ah yeah?

Hence why the review.  But it somehow is too hard for them to work around with. Hope that $350 gets you a beer guys. Not about money but principle!!

Do better next time will ya
Nice friendly camp site up on the Cape.

Great place, great value, welcoming and friendly helpful staff, close to the beaches, if you want to escape the busy camp grounds of the town then 100% recommended. Only downfall was the showers are a bit small with little people but other then that its best camp ground in Exmouth by far. Thanks for having us and we will be back in the next few months.

Don't stay here if you can avoid it. The management has absolutely atrocious manners and swear at you. Sharon must be the rudest hospitality staff ive encounterd. It's over priced for what your getting and no electricity for a paid powered sites. They stalk your every move also. My advice is to stay in town and travel the small distance to see the sights. Far better accomodation and value for money without as many flies to boot.

Endroit authentique, parfait pour une déconnexion loin de la civilisation avec aucune lumière le soir. Nous avons passé une super semaine avec nos jeunes enfants en tente grâce à la piscine et à la proximité de plages de rêves à 15min! Nous recommandons pour ceux qui aiment bien la nature !
Pretty windy, has a pool which made it extremely worth it. Lots of good beaches within 20 mins

place was fake advertised the cabin i stayed in was in horrific condition with nothing cleaned, owners were rude and disrespectful and we ended up leaving to a place closer to town and a lot cleaner and nicer

Place looks so run down and it’s far away from convenience stores. We arrived there, took a look around and decided nope. We’d rather go somewhere else.
For the price you pay, you’re much better off staying at RAC Exmouth.

Awful management, not told till last minute that it’s a low powered site so good luck using your aircon in your vans, overpriced rude staff better of staying at RAC can use all your facilities including your AC and about half the price of homestead, stay in town and  just drive to all the lovely local spots in Exmouth than waste your money for overpriced rudeness.
Owners are rude and screamed at a 16 year old girl for no reason
Great location, however old ablution block needs replacing. Have stayed he several times . Disappointed Cafe closed towards the end of the school holidays.
Great location, clean facilities, reasonably priced
Better than most caravan parks but a drive to any swimming (other than thier pool). Good place to see the coast.
We didn't stay. We dropped into the cafe for lunch and after a holiday spanning 15000km, these were the best chips I have had anywhere

Lovely receptionist. We were pleased to get a tree for shade. Very dusty and dry. Nice pool. Could have done with more toilets/ showers. Very basic  camp kitchen. No electricity connected. Good for people who like to fish as close to boat ramp.

Un bon campground simple, bien situé pour explorer la côte ouest vers le sud. tToute petite piscine, sanitaires propres et efficaces. On a eu la chance d’avoir un emplacement avec un peu d’ombre. Électricité limitée: pas d’air conditionnée, pas de grille pain, pas de bouilloire. Réservation de sortie snorkeling possibles à l’accueil comme la location de l’équipement.
Loved our stay here .

I don’t know what I liked more, the campsite we got near the entrance with cars and boats going in and out at 5am or the smell of the fish guts on the fly blown bin 10 metres from our site. We booked days in advance and this was the crap we got. As for the back amenities block it’s like something out of a third world ghetto

This is a good caravan park but my visit was marred by 4 days of wind.
Unisex bathrooms, great idea but shower cubicles are too small.
Limited power and unable to stay connected to water was annoying.  I requested a site with shade and got the Sahara desert, NO TREES NO GRASS
As long as demand exceeds supply it will stay like this and be expensive.

We loved staying at Yardie Homestead, it was super welcoming and friendly - so good we ended up booking our final two nights here. We stayed on the grass patch camping the first time and that was very accessible. The second time we stayed on the outskirts which was tougher dirt, but recommend this if you want to back onto the wilderness rather than feel like you are in the middle of a campsite. It's a brilliant location for accessing Cape Range NP/

First impression, "Last Stop Larrimah". Last impression, "Last Stop Larrimah". For $220 per night, we stayed in the modified donga accommodation which was suffering from a lack of maintenance, the shower constantly dribbled and all the metal framing and guttering to the building and verandah hadn't had a lick of paint since the original and all were rusting badly. The accommodation was advertised as having power but this was restricted, there's not enough power to use a toaster, kettle or even a small coffee pod machine. On a positive note to finish off, Sharon at reception was sensational, very friendly and helpful, also the couple from Margaret River who cooked and ran the cafe were also great.
We loved our stay at Yardie and will definitely be back

My partner and I stayed at Yardie Homestay for our honeymoon, and we couldn't have chosen a better place. From the moment we arrived, the reception staff particularly Sharon, they were incredibly helpful and genuinely looked after us, always greeting us with a smile.

The homestay offers all the amenities you need for a comfortable stay, including hot, warm showers and convenient laundry facilities. We also appreciated the friendly atmosphere around the site, with other guests adding to the welcoming environment. One of our favorite parts was the cafe, where we enjoyed decent coffee each morning, which was a nice touch.

Yardie Homestay is also ideally located, making it very handy for exploring nearby attractions, such as the stunning National Park, which is a must-visit.

Overall, Yardie Homestay made our honeymoon truly special, and we would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a relaxing and friendly place to stay.
It needs lots of TLC.

We came to Yardie with our wedding group in tow (group of approx 30) and the team were impeccable. So very accommodating, they knew who we were and would always say hello in passing, which made us and our entire group feel so very welcomed.
Camp site was huge - enough to fit a camper, tent, jetski and ute on whilst still having a comfortable sitting area.
Facilities were clean and well kept for a Homestead stay - nothing fancy but worked perfectly.

We could not recommend Yardie Homestead enough. We will definitely be back

We really enjoyed our stay here, the reception staff were very friendly and helpful and it is brilliantly located near the snorkeling spots. The wifi worked well in the bbq area and even stretched to our tent in the unpowered area which was great.

The amenities are quite old but were clean each time we used them. There was also plenty of fridge space in the bbq area!

We used Yardie Creek Homestead as a base for an event, booked out 4 cabins and at least 7 sites…the staff was wonderful and accomodating. The location was awesome and with the cafe onsite we could catch up and relax
At the start of the national park it was great for us to visit all the places that Exmouth is so well known for
Thanks Yardie Creek Homestead

Females be aware! James the owner is a creep and will not hesitate to swear at you. He is a small man who doesn’t know how to express himself self on an intellectual level, so just resorts to swearing and getting angry. Good on you champ.

This caravan park is disgusting. They treat the customers extremely rudely and since leaving  I have had multiple conversations with people who have had similar experiences. They will kick you out for so much as farting past 9pm and take your money. I am honestly gob smacked about the way they treated my friends and I. The owners of Yardi Creek go out of their way to foster a hostile and unwelcoming environment.
Big Thanks to Ange for all her help booking us in at Yardie Homestead. Dinner from the Cafe was delicious, nice hot showers and super friendly staff!

We LOVED Yardie Homestead! We would come back here in a heartbeat! One of the only places in the last 6 weeks of travel where we have had hot showers at all times of the day! Pristinely clean, flat sandy large sites. Great cafe from 7am - 8pm, good coffee! Kids loved the pool. Very pet friendly, staff super helpful! And of course LOCATION LOCATION LOCATION!  Closed caravan park to Nigaloo Reef outside of the caravan park with beach’s 5min away, snorkeling sites a 15min drive! Wish we could have stayed longer. Book in advance they are super popular with good reason! Thank you Yardie for a brilliant stay!

Great place to stay, especially for travelling into the National Park daily. Older ablution blocks, but staff kept them clean. Caravan sites are huge. There are plenty of powered and uncovered sites. Cafe open 12 plus hours per day. Playground and tennis court to entertain the kids. The swimming pool is also available for the days the beach is too windy.
Terrible place.
Ange & Sharon are the worst receptionist! Sold our booking to someone else. How can you even do that? Such a shame.
Nice place to stay with caravan. Great Cafe . Amenities need upgrading but they are clean.

Yardie Homestead is my favourite place to stay in Exmouth. It offers proximity to the national park and beaches without having to make the 30 min trip from town each day. The facilities are awesome and the restaurant offers some good options for food and coffees in the morning.
Really like this campsite. They had a singer on in the evening when we arrived which was lovely. Showers were pretty dark though

We had such a fantastic week long stay at Yardie Homestead, and were so sad to leave. It really did feel like a home away from home. We came as a group of 4 families: 2 camping and 2 in cabins and we all loved the place. It's in the perfect spot for exploring the national park but not too far from town either. The sunsets and starry nights are out of this world. Beautiful drinking water, spacious camp sites and fast free internet (at the bbq area). Yes the bathrooms are a bit dated but they were clean and showers were hot. The onsite cafe makes great coffee and we loved the live music and bonfire nights. The communal kitchens are basic with just a gas hob and hot plate so come with meals in mind to cook on that! We all agree we wouldn't stay anywhere else in Exmouth, and look forward to returning.

Overall the best place to stay that allows dogs. Right side of Exmouth to access all the great snorkel spots. Decent size caravan sites, bathrooms desperately need updating, water quality was great, the food at the cafe was a little pricey but very delicious and good quality! They have powered sites but you can’t run anything with a ‘heating element’ - no toasters, electric kettles, air conditioners etc.

It was great to be back for another stay at Yardie Homestead! Beautiful spacious camp site, clean facilities, great location central between cape range and town, amazing food and live music provided a great atmosphere at the cafe, good coffee and friendly staff! Tennis court and swimming pool a bonus too! Thanks to all of the lovely Yardie team, we’ll be back next year!
Decent campsite - well-located with good facilities.

We stayed 2 nights on a powered caravan site, the caravan park is dog friendly.
We have a 22ft van & a RAM truck, plenty of space for both.
Cafe is quirky, with great coffee & food options if you need & it’s overlooking a medium size pool, & nice grassed area with picnic tables to sit and relax.
Definitely nice enough to recommend.
Excellent place to stay to explore the ningaloo reef.

Loved the location
Wasn't expecting the Ritz but
Amenities  could  be given a bit more attention  ... I feel   with volume of people in park need to be cleaned / monitored more frequently

We had a fantastic four nights here in May with our 3 kids all under 4 years of age. I mention this as it was the main reason for choosing here, instead of being off grid in Cape Range National Park. We still wanted to have water and power. The restrictions on power were no issue this time of year as the weather was beautiful and we didn't need air con.  You can't use appliances with a heating element either, for example your kettle or hot water system on electric. Water is readily available without leaving your site however a single tap is shared between multiple sites. As such, you fill your tanks then disconnect your hose. I'll add that you can do this as many times as you need. The water is very palatable so no need to BYO drinking water. There's a laundry, ablution block, dump point, fish cleaning station, boat washing, beautiful swimming pool, grassy area to relax with picnic tables by the pool area. Theres also a playground and tennis court. Theres a cafe which did great pizza, hot chips, coffees and others things. Plenty of shade around the place, the shop does fuel and there's telstra phone reception. Five minutes from Tantabiddi boat ramp.  We thoroughly enjoyed it here and will return.

Worst customer service I have ever witnessed. Older lady with glasses made an double booking on our camping spot. I made online booking for 2 nights and when i arrived on site also booked for third night. On second night someone were on our camping spot with campervan. We figured out that they made a double booking and tried to call phone number which was on the paper they give us on site. Nobody answered because it was already 7pm.
In the morning went to office and ask about it and there was that same woman who made our booking. I asked her about why there was someone on our camping spot and also ask can I get any refund for that. No apologies or anything, she just said its an double booking but that it is our mistake because we havent call her about the situation. We said that we call the number which was on the paper she gave us, but she said we should have call some emergency number and because we didn't do that it is our mistake :D she never told us about that kind of number. Only number we can see is on the paper she gave us.
I asked is there any place to give feedback of this situation and then she went crazy and said we are threatening her and have to leave this campsite and she will give the refund for next night back.
We had to be careful what we say next because we were going to see whalesharks next day and everything were full booked. We had little talk and then she gave us our spot where to sleep next night.
After that we walk off the office and saw our neighbours who were sleeping next to us and tell the situation what happened. That woman came out of the office and started following us and listening our conversation.
Sadly in Exmouth there is every campgrounds full booked. Even though campground is alright, but customer service would need an big upgrade.
All the Australians we have met during our travel have been no worries and easygoing good people, this was the first bad apple.

Great location to access Turquoise Bay, Oyster Stacks and Lakeside. Ammenities are a bit old, water available to fill tanks but not stay connected, and power is 6 amps. Staff friendly and helpful, coffee is very good, we loved our stay as saves an extra 30 minute drive from Exmouth.

$50 per powered site, however not allowed to use air-conditioning,  hot water unless you use your gas, no microwave or electrical appliances.  Why have powered sites. Can't direct hookup to water cause pressure problems however can keep filling your tanks.

I just mucked up and booked wrong dates online. Called up and left a message explaining the situation. Got a call back within minutes and my stuff up was resolved very quickly and without hassle. THANK YOU YARDIE!!! (And Jess in reception, lifesaver.)
Such a peaceful place with spacious sites

We were very comfortable here at Yardie. Coming off grid it was a joy having showers and fresh water. We don't have air fryers air conditioners and coffee machines so power restrictions did not worry us.
A big thank you to the staff in the cafe. Food was amazing! All staff were friendly.
Amenities a bit dated but location location location

Great spot to base yourself on visiting the local area and national park, fuel station on site, as well as a cafe and pool. Amenities were ok . If you have a boat, there is a cleaning station, and you are  close to a few boat ramps. Bare in mind, tho we weren't able to use a kettle air fryer or any high-powered electric device or AC. Which wasn't great.
really really shiddy, dont go.
Simply a great place to stay

This is an exceptional place to stay to enjoy the Ningaloo coast. The facilities are good, staff are excellent and the onsite cafe is a real bonus. It is made very clear that power is limited & that you can easily run your fridge & freezer but not items which draw large current like AC units or electric kettles. Highly recommended & we wished we could have stayed longer. Plus our dogs were very welcome too

Freundliche Bedienung.
Ruhiger Ort.
Ideal als Standort für Ausflüge zum Strand.
Netter Pool.

We were under the impression that when a park advertises power that is what you get!!!
Power here means it is available for lights and apparently your fridge. We had to use gas for the fridge. If i had known we would not have booked, as it was stinking hot!!!
On the up side the location was great for the national park.
Bathrooms old but clean.
Swimming pool was a welcome relief from the heat.
We asked for a refund for our last night. Declined….. 35 days required!!!!
At $50 per night , it was abit rich!!!
I would not return or recommend.
The receptionist was lovely

PLEASE NOTE THEY DON'T ALLOW 2 VEHICLES PER SITE. They do not mention this at all and will ask you to leave your car in the carpark, which we obliged. Even if other people next to you have 2 vehicles, they do not care. I was under the impression rules are rules but not so. Be aware of the staff as they don't care what-so-ever about how you have come prepared. We were as nice as possible about the situation but ended up leaving the park half way through our stay (3 days early) because of the ignorance. Terrible experience.

Warning ⚠️ This site is falsely advertised as a powered site. It is, in fact, not one. Very limited. My daughter booked and payed in advance for the site.
Once she found out it wasn't powered, she phoned to cancel. She has recently had a kidney transplant and it is vital that she has a powered site. She needs the aircon on to keep her medication at the correct temperature.  She also requires boiling water and other essentials that require electricity. She explained this to the owner . I also called to explain but on both occasions we were met with hostility and a complete lack of empathy. They said to read the fine print. They were so rude and disrespectful and their ONLY goal was to hang on to their money. Hope they can sleep at night.

Easier access to many places.
Pool and cafe is great.
Bring strong pegs.
Washing is expensive.

While the park facilities are very dated, the location is amazing. The track towards the ranges is lovely, but be Dingo aware as they have been sighted in the area. The swimming pool is an oasis from the heat, as airconditioners can't be run due to power limitations. Water is available and good, but you can't be continually hooked up and must replenish your tanks as you go from a single tap shared between adjacent sites. Overall I would stay there again if traveling up the west coast.
We stayed on the campground and had a great stay! The bathrooms are nice, there are washing machines and a pool, would recommend!!

Sehr schöner Campingplatz. Jedoch darf man die Klimanlage (Air Con nicht laufen lassen im Wohnmobil was bei 45 Grad Außentemperatur sehr dumm ist. Schade das Sie es nicht schaffen genügend Strom bereitzustellen für ihre Gäste. Daher 2Sterne Abzug. Ansonsten Top!

The main amenities block was dilapidated needing an urgent upgrade like the ones for the tour groups. Sites have shared tap water and you have turn off any element (hot water in your van) because of the park's limited power output.
.

Camped at Yardie Homestead and it's advisable to book ahead—we lucked out with the last site. The laundry was a bit pricey at $5, and no dryers, but ample clothesline space made up for it. The pool was a nice touch, and they offered evening entertainment. There's also a restaurant if you prefer dining in. Overall, a decent spot with some amenities, but booking in advance is key.

De locatie is geweldig voor snorkelen en bezoek aan cape Range National Park. De camping heeft naar mijn mening echter maar 1 fijne plaats om te slapen en dat is campground 1 voor tenten. De enige plek met gras en genoeg bomen voor schaduw.

Location fantastic. People friendly. Good cafe with long opening hours.   pool for kids. Showers and toilets are as well good.

Kitchens are poor. One of the 2 kitchens had only stoves. None of them had a microwave. Washing machine program running only 30min, too short for the red dirt. Shop could have a bit more, like muesli and pegs for washing lines.

Great camping grounds very basic has a couple of BBQ areas and shower/toilet blocks and laundry. No microwaves on site has small pool and Cafe. Best thing is the location being so close to all the best snorkelling and beaches.
Excellent location.  Very clean.  Hot showers running fresh water and electricity.  No aircon or heating allowed but that’s fine.

I would not ever recommend this place…unless you like to be squashed in like a can of sardines…get charged 50 dollars a night…for what…lots of noise…no power…no water..can’t boil a kettle..can’t use a toaster…ablution block full of flies and dirty…

Nice park out of town great location for ningaloo reef.  There are really old ablutions on site that need to be removed a real eyesore but they do have clean ablution bring tie downs as it's extremely windy. The only real gripe I have is that they advertise powered sites but when you arrive you get told you can't use anything with a heating element so no hot water systems toaster kettle the only thing the power is good for is your lights and fridge microwave which if you have modern caravan you can run anyway without needing power, and you are not allowed to keep your water tanks plugged into mains supply you must use your tank water 1st so in saying that $50 a night is quiet expensive. Pool and small  Cafe on site
Wilderness camp, but an oasis in this area. Close to beautiful snorkeling areas.

Great stay, just outside of Exmouth close to the beaches on the west of Exmouth.  We stayed at a powered site, the powered sites had power restrictions so could only run small appliances no aircon sadly. Toilets and showers were old but 100% clean and hot water was great. Also the cafe had amazing chips 10/10

Great park, close to jetty, Mandu Mandu Gorge & Yardie Creek. Sites were a bit small, but great facilities, cafe/restaurant & pool. Older amenities but clean & tidy. Very busy park, always fully booked. Definitely recommend it.

Great place to stay. Staff behind the counter were very helpful. We read the reviews so knew what to expect but I do agree that thier cancellation policy is way too strict (especially when always booked out). Nothing was said when booking about the limitations of a powered site. We knew, so that was OK for us... but it should be disclosed over the phone. Three bathroom blocks, 2 were pretty good but one should be condemmed (men). The rest of the facilities were great. If you know what your getting beforehand I'm sure you will love this place.

We didn't stay, just called in for a coffee and ice cream. A popular, laid back, simple caravan park in an excellent location for the New Nigaloo reef beaches down the road or a 20+ min drive into the town of Exmouth. Very friendly staff. Appeared to be a fair few families staying at the park and alot of retired folk.
Good location on the coast, nice big sites, good amenities but very dusty. Was very windy when we weren't there.

Staff and Cleaners are good . Management very poor we witnessed a lady in her 60s who was assigned to tent area 1 try to move to tent area 2  because of area 1 having dogs and she was scared of them and traveling alone and just competed a 8 hr drive from Tom price to be confronted by management and abruptly told to move back and there was no option she then was told she could go to office where the  manager was waiting this was 6pm she went back to explain to management her predicament and was swiftly told there’s no option you will get a full refund and leave … This lady was so scared of dogs she packed her car and slept out on the road that night in her car and the guests who witnessed this rallied behind her checking on her during the  night and drove her back in to shower in the morning and make her a hot breakfast while finding her a cancellation booking in town costing her over $1100. Area2  was not fully booked as management tried to use they basically kicked a lady out into the street by herself It seems some caravan camping parks and the boom they are experiencing is going not only to there pockets but there head …. I suggest they be very careful when the tide turns these actions will be remembered

Best place we’ve camped before, always made room for us last minute if we couldn’t find anything else, the staff are so so friendly, reception and cafe, also best coffee in Exmouth (we tried lots) can’t wait to come back! Thanks so much yardie crew ❤️

Un très bons camping bien perdu comme on aime simple mais avec de très bonnes installations et le café ouvert à 7h qui passe bien. La cuisine du camp est aussi très sympa et bien équipée, on a bcp apprécié notre séjour et très bon emplacement pour aller au cape range national Park
great little spot to set up your camper in the shade and still be nearby to the beautiful beaches along the cape range.

This was our second time to Exmouth, and we would definitely stay here again. The location beats being in town. You are already there on the Ningaloo Coast side. Perfect location for quick trips to the Reef.
Big powered sites, they ask you to fill your tanks up and not have them hooked up. Just go with it, they are on tank water so water is precious.
Facilities are great. Its a homestead, it was awesome. We will be back.

More than alright for a few nights. It's a large campground with plenty of unpowered/powered sites. They also have a nice grass patch for tents only. Staff is really friendly and willing to help. They have snorkel gear up for hire and some small goods for really fair prices.
Great amenity blocks, always clean and looked after. Surely they aren't brand new and modern but they do their job. I actually appreciate the privacy of having my "own" bathroom.
The only thing I'd have to criticize is the kitchen. There's two of them and both are far too small for the amount of guests. There's barely any space to sit and there were always people in line to do their dishes or waiting to use the cook top. The kitchen on our end didn't have a single fridge so every single time we needed something we had to walk 10 minutes since our spot was all the way at the end. Pretty annoying.
But overall I can't be too harsh on this place. It's a bit pricey for what you get but it's the same all over the country right now. I really love the location as it's out of town and close to the beaches and the national park. It was my second stay and I'd gladly would come back again.
Cool camping place
We were here for one night, would have loved to stay longer and explore, looked amazing, a very busy place
Managed to get squeezed in for one night in the busiest time of the year. Big thank you to Yardie!

Lucky to get 1 night here. Footy fans made camp kitchen uncomfortable to cook and eat while they commandeered the tables while a match was on. Unlucky, yes, but come on victorians,  be reasonable to people who have to use the facilities and have no interest in afl.

It is a great caravan park close to beautiful beaches and of course the nearby Cape Range National Park.
Snorkelling in the park is fantastic, we loved the Oyster Stacks, Turquoise Bay and Lakeside.
Hiking around Mandu Mandu was challenging but worth seeing the Rock Wallabies and Eagles.
Best spot to stay on west side if your not self sufficient.

NICHT ZU EMPFEHLEN !!

Dreckig, laut und kaum liebe investiert. Die Sanitäten Anlagen lassen zu wünschen übrig. Die Menschen dort sind unfreundlich und die anderen Camper liefen permanent durch unser Camp, wie selbstverständlich. Die Campkitchen bietet kaum Möglichkeiten zu kochen. Ein Kühlschrank wird zwar angeboten doch es hängt ein Zettel dran "man solle ihn bitte nicht nutzen".

Wir reisen von Camp zu Camp und haben auf unserem Wege nichts vergleichbar schlechtes gefunden.
A wonderful wilderness campsite - relaxed clean and not trying to be tropical ! Keeping the desert to reef experience real.

$50 per night,you pay a premium for the isolation book months if not years ahead,did not have a good time here so cannot rate it-i will forever call it Vomit city ,felt like being gouged for money at every turn 🤮

What a great little spot to camp in Exmouth!

Lots of space per camp site and has a nice feeling about it. Facilities have been there a while but do the job great. Showers are good & were never busy while we were there.

It’s 15 mins to Exmouth town and super close to all of the great spots!
Very dissapointed to follow the cafe open sign, only to find the cafe isn't open at all for lunches
